Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Does this patch actually allow creation of kfi peers?

Patch Set 2:

> Does this patch actually allow creation of kfi peers?

I mean, that's the intent... is it not working for you?

Patch Set 2:

> > Does this patch actually allow creation of kfi peers?
 > 
 > I mean, that's the intent... is it not working for you?

I haven't tried it, and test scripts aren't ported, but it looks like lnet_expr2ips in b2_12 only recognizes o2ib and tcp nids

Patch Set 2:

lnet_expr2ips ??? I don't see this function.

Patch Set 2:

This is the error I get when adding a peer with kfi nid:

# lnetctl peer add --prim_nid=6.6.6.6@tcp --nid 4@gni
add:
    - peer_ni:
          errno: -8
          descr: "Success"
# lnetctl peer add --prim_nid=6.6.6.5@tcp --nid 4@kfi
add:
    - peer_ni:
          errno: -8
          descr: "Success"
# lnetctl peer show
peer:
    - primary nid: 6.6.6.5@tcp
      Multi-Rail: True
      peer ni:
        - nid: 6.6.6.5@tcp
          state: NA
    - primary nid: 6.6.6.6@tcp
      Multi-Rail: True
      peer ni:
        - nid: 6.6.6.6@tcp
          state: NA

Patch Set 2:

> lnet_expr2ips ??? I don't see this function.

It is in lnet/utils/lnetconfig/liblnetconfig.c and appears to be in part the cause why kfi nid is rejected

Patch Set 2:

As an aside (this is just my "clueless" misunderstanding), why does it show:

 add:
    - peer_ni:
          errno: -8
          descr: "Success"

as returning an error -8 with the description "Success"?  AFAIK, -8 = -ENOEXEC = "Exec format error".

Patch Set 2:

Nice catch, Serguei.

I had forgotten that DLC is broken with numeric NIDs in b2_12. This was fixed under LU-12410.

Andreas, any thought whether it is worthwhile to backport those changes to b2_12? I don't know if y'all are planning many more 2.12 releases.

Serguei, this patch still has value on its own as it adds support for kfilnd network type to core lnet. This allows us to create routes to kfi networks and understand kfi NIDs. Those are more important than full support for numeric NIDs with lnetctl/DLC.

Patch Set 2:

Chris, there will almost certainly be a 2.12.10 release before we stop on that branch.

I definitely think it is worthwhile to include this into the final 2.12.10 release, as this will be the last one in that vintage and some customers might be OK to upgrade to a 2.12.10, but not 2.15.x for a long time (or ever).  That is mostly of benefit to HPE, since this will only affect KFI systems, or access to them from other networks.

Patch Set 2:

> Chris, there will almost certainly be a 2.12.10 release before we
 > stop on that branch.
 > 
 > I definitely think it is worthwhile to include this into the final
 > 2.12.10 release, as this will be the last one in that vintage and
 > some customers might be OK to upgrade to a 2.12.10, but not 2.15.x
 > for a long time (or ever).  That is mostly of benefit to HPE, since
 > this will only affect KFI systems, or access to them from other
 > networks.

Sounds good. I'll work on the backport.

Patch Set 2:

Serguei would you reconsider your -1 in the meantime?

Patch Set 2:

> Serguei would you reconsider your -1 in the meantime?

^ Serguei?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Yes, sorry, I have not been getting gerrit email notifications at the time.

Patch Set 2:

I've pushed the LU-12410 patch series for b2_12 on top of this patch.

Abandoned

Patch hasn't landed for over a year, so abandon it.

commit.subject: LU-15983 lnet: Define KFILND network type
commit.message:
LU-15983 lnet: Define KFILND network type

Define the KFILND network type. This reserves the network type number
for future implementation and allows creation of kfi peers and
adding routes to kfi peers.

Lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/47830
Lustre-commit: 5fea36c952373c9a235be7bf57eb2e516fcb36b2

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
HPE-bug-id: LUS-11060
Signed-off-by: Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I9111645f1290c8af4937d1b2689a068df81922a4
